forward_patch:
--- original +++ modified @@ -0,0 +1,89 @@ +--- +assignees: +- wballard +position_column: todo +position_ordinal: b180 +title: Grid cell hit area = full cell via Field padding prop (drop redundant GridCellFocusable scope) +--- +## What + +In the grid view, keyboard focus (and the focus indicator) only covers the inner content of each cell, not the visible cell box. The fix is **not** to add yet another zone wrapping the cell ‚Äî that would compound the existing redundant-zone problem. Instead, give `Field` the ability to own its own padding so its existing registered `FocusZone` border-box becomes the full cell box. + +### Today's structure (the problem) + +`kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx:931-943` (`GridCellFocusable`): + +```tsx + + +
+ {children /* */} +
+ + +``` + +And cell padding lives on the outer ``: + +```tsx +const cellClasses = cn("px-3 py-1.5 align-middle max-w-[300px]", ci === 0 && "pl-4", ‚Ä¶); +``` + +Two problems: + +1. **Padding outside the registered rect.** The kernel reads `getBoundingClientRect()` on the FocusScope host (`focus-scope.tsx:322-340`) ‚Äî which is the inner unpadded `
`. Padding sits outside, so the focus rect is smaller than the visible cell. +2. **Redundant zone.** `` already renders its own `` (`field.tsx:621-628`). `GridCellFocusable` wraps it in *another* `` ‚Äî two registrations per cell, exactly the duplication the user wants eliminated. + +### Fix direction + +1. **Add an optional `padding` (or `containerClassName` ‚Äî see decision note) prop to `Field`** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx`. Forward it to the className on Field's `` host element. Default empty ‚Üí behavior unchanged for every existing caller. +2. **Remove `GridCellFocusable`'s `` wrapping in `data-table.tsx`** (or fold it down so the cell registers a single zone ‚Äî Field's own). Pass the `px-3 py-1.5` (and `pl-4` for first column) padding via the new Field prop instead of via ``. +3. The cell's outer `` becomes a bare layout container (no padding). Click/dblclick handlers and `data-cell-cursor` move to whatever element survives the consolidation ‚Äî preserve the existing onClick/onDoubleClick semantics. + +Result: one zone per cell (Field's own), border-box equals the full visible cell box, focus rect covers padding. No redundant nesting. + +### Decision note (for implementer) + +Decide between: + +- (a) `padding?: string` ‚Äî typed narrowly to a Tailwind padding class, communicates intent. +- (b) `containerClassName?: string` ‚Äî generic forwarding seam onto the FocusZone host. + +(a) is more opinionated and matches the user's request literally; (b) is more general but invites misuse. **Recommend (a)** unless the implementer finds another grid-like consumer that needs non-padding layout classes ‚Äî keep the seam narrow. + +### Non-goals + +- Do **not** add another outer `FocusScope` or `FocusZone` around the cell. The whole point of this task is to *eliminate* a redundant zone, not add one. +- Do **not** change Field's behavior for non-grid callers (Inspector rows, `` cells, nav-bar, board-selector, column-view title). Default empty padding preserves their layout. +- Do **not** introduce a `padding` prop that bypasses `` (e.g. wraps it in another padded div) ‚Äî the padding MUST land on the FocusZone's host element so `getBoundingClientRect` includes it. + +### Files to modify + +- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` ‚Äî add the `padding` prop to the `FieldProps` interface (~line 213-276), forward it to `` (~line 621-628). FocusZone already accepts className passthrough (`focus-zone.tsx:141-142, 499-516`). +- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx` ‚Äî collapse `GridCellFocusable` so each cell renders a single registered zone via ``. Move `px-3 py-1.5` (and `pl-4` for column 0) off `` and into the `padding` prop. Preserve `cellCursorAttr`, click, and double-click semantics. +- (Possibly) `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` ‚Äî if cell wiring also lives there, mirror the change. + +## Acceptance Criteria + +- [ ] Pressing Tab/arrow into a grid cell: the focus indicator rect equals the **full visible cell box** (including the padding currently on ``), not just the inner content. +- [ ] Clicking anywhere within a cell's padding focuses that cell (bigger hit area). +- [ ] Each grid cell registers exactly **one** spatial scope (Field's own ``) ‚Äî verified by counting `spatial_register_scope`/`spatial_register_zone` calls per cell render. +- [ ] No regression for non-grid Field consumers: Inspector rows, `` cells, nav-bar, etc. visually and behaviorally unchanged (default `padding` empty/undefined). +- [ ] Existing grid keyboard-nav tests (`grid-view.spatial-nav.test.tsx`, `grid-view.nav-is-eventdriven.test.tsx`) still pass. + +## Tests + +- [ ] **TDD: write the failing unit test first** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.spatial-nav.test.tsx`. Render `` and assert the captured `spatial_register_zone` rect width/height includes the padding (compare to the same Field without `padding`). Run, confirm it fails, then implement the prop. +- [ ] Add a regression test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.test.tsx` (or a new `grid-view.cell-hit-area.spatial.test.tsx` matching project naming) that: + - Renders a grid with N rows √ó M columns. + - Asserts each cell registers exactly one focus scope (no `GridCellFocusable` outer scope). + - Asserts the registered rect's width/height matches the visible `` border-box. +- [ ] Add a click-padding test: click on the padding area of a cell and assert the cell receives focus (use `getBoundingClientRect()` on the rendered `` and dispatch a click at `rect.x + 2, rect.y + 2`). +- [ ] Run: `bun test field.spatial-nav.test.tsx` ‚Äî green. +- [ ] Run: `bun test data-table` and `bun test grid-view` ‚Äî green (no regressions). +- [ ] Run: `bun test` for the spatial-nav e2e suite ‚Äî green. + +## Workflow + +- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî failing test first (Field with padding registers larger rect), implement the prop, then collapse `GridCellFocusable`. +- After the prop lands, audit other Field call sites (Inspector, EntityCard, nav-bar, board-selector, column-view title) to confirm none accidentally need it ‚Äî opt-in only. \ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What In the grid view, keyboard focus (and the focus indicator) only covers the inner content of each cell, not the visible cell box. The fix is **not** to add yet another zone wrapping the cell ‚Äî that would compound the existing redundant-zone problem. Instead, give `Field` the ability to own its own padding so its existing registered `FocusZone` border-box becomes the full cell box. ### Today's structure (the problem) `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx:931-943` (`GridCellFocusable`): ```tsx
{children /* */}
``` And cell padding lives on the outer ``: ```tsx const cellClasses = cn("px-3 py-1.5 align-middle max-w-[300px]", ci === 0 && "pl-4", ‚Ä¶); ``` Two problems: 1. **Padding outside the registered rect.** The kernel reads `getBoundingClientRect()` on the FocusScope host (`focus-scope.tsx:322-340`) ‚Äî which is the inner unpadded `
`. Padding sits outside, so the focus rect is smaller than the visible cell. 2. **Redundant zone.** `` already renders its own `` (`field.tsx:621-628`). `GridCellFocusable` wraps it in *another* `` ‚Äî two registrations per cell, exactly the duplication the user wants eliminated. ### Fix direction 1. **Add an optional `padding` (or `containerClassName` ‚Äî see decision note) prop to `Field`** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx`. Forward it to the className on Field's `` host element. Default empty ‚Üí behavior unchanged for every existing caller. 2. **Remove `GridCellFocusable`'s `` wrapping in `data-table.tsx`** (or fold it down so the cell registers a single zone ‚Äî Field's own). Pass the `px-3 py-1.5` (and `pl-4` for first column) padding via the new Field prop instead of via ``. 3. The cell's outer `` becomes a bare layout container (no padding). Click/dblclick handlers and `data-cell-cursor` move to whatever element survives the consolidation ‚Äî preserve the existing onClick/onDoubleClick semantics. Result: one zone per cell (Field's own), border-box equals the full visible cell box, focus rect covers padding. No redundant nesting. ### Decision note (for implementer) Decide between: - (a) `padding?: string` ‚Äî typed narrowly to a Tailwind padding class, communicates intent. - (b) `containerClassName?: string` ‚Äî generic forwarding seam onto the FocusZone host. (a) is more opinionated and matches the user's request literally; (b) is more general but invites misuse. **Recommend (a)** unless the implementer finds another grid-like consumer that needs non-padding layout classes ‚Äî keep the seam narrow. ### Non-goals - Do **not** add another outer `FocusScope` or `FocusZone` around the cell. The whole point of this task is to *eliminate* a redundant zone, not add one. - Do **not** change Field's behavior for non-grid callers (Inspector rows, `` cells, nav-bar, board-selector, column-view title). Default empty padding preserves their layout. - Do **not** introduce a `padding` prop that bypasses `` (e.g. wraps it in another padded div) ‚Äî the padding MUST land on the FocusZone's host element so `getBoundingClientRect` includes it. ### Files to modify - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` ‚Äî add the `padding` prop to the `FieldProps` interface (~line 213-276), forward it to `` (~line 621-628). FocusZone already accepts className passthrough (`focus-zone.tsx:141-142, 499-516`). - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx` ‚Äî collapse `GridCellFocusable` so each cell renders a single registered zone via ``. Move `px-3 py-1.5` (and `pl-4` for column 0) off `` and into the `padding` prop. Preserve `cellCursorAttr`, click, and double-click semantics. - (Possibly) `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` ‚Äî if cell wiring also lives there, mirror the change. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Pressing Tab/arrow into a grid cell: the focus indicator rect equals the **full visible cell box** (including the padding currently on ``), not just the inner content. - [ ] Clicking anywhere within a cell's padding focuses that cell (bigger hit area). - [ ] Each grid cell registers exactly **one** spatial scope (Field's own ``) ‚Äî verified by counting `spatial_register_scope`/`spatial_register_zone` calls per cell render. - [ ] No regression for non-grid Field consumers: Inspector rows, `` cells, nav-bar, etc. visually and behaviorally unchanged (default `padding` empty/undefined). - [ ] Existing grid keyboard-nav tests (`grid-view.spatial-nav.test.tsx`, `grid-view.nav-is-eventdriven.test.tsx`) still pass. ## Tests - [ ] **TDD: write the failing unit test first** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.spatial-nav.test.tsx`. Render `` and assert the captured `spatial_register_zone` rect width/height includes the padding (compare to the same Field without `padding`). Run, confirm it fails, then implement the prop. - [ ] Add a regression test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.test.tsx` (or a new `grid-view.cell-hit-area.spatial.test.tsx` matching project naming) that: - Renders a grid with N rows √ó M columns. - Asserts each cell registers exactly one focus scope (no `GridCellFocusable` outer scope). - Asserts the registered rect's width/height matches the visible `` border-box. - [ ] Add a click-padding test: click on the padding area of a cell and assert the cell receives focus (use `getBoundingClientRect()` on the rendered `` and dispatch a click at `rect.x + 2, rect.y + 2`). - [ ] Run: `bun test field.spatial-nav.test.tsx` ‚Äî green. - [ ] Run: `bun test data-table` and `bun test grid-view` ‚Äî green (no regressions). - [ ] Run: `bun test` for the spatial-nav e2e suite ‚Äî green. ## Workflow - Use `/tdd` ‚Äî failing test first (Field with padding registers larger rect), implement the prop, then collapse `GridCellFocusable`. - After the prop lands, audit other Field call sites (Inspector, EntityCard, nav-bar, board-selector, column-view title) to confirm none accidentally need it ‚Äî opt-in only.
Grid cell hit area = full cell via Field padding prop (drop redundant GridCellFocusable scope)
